From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Estonian long-distance runner

Lily Luik

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Luik at the 2016 Olympics | |
| Personal information | |
| Born | (1985-10-14) 14 October 1985 (age 40) |
| Height | 1.63 m (5 ft 4 in)[[1]](#cite_note-rio-1) |
| Weight | 48 kg (106 lb) |
| Sport | |
| Country | Estonia |
| Sport | [Track and field](/wiki/Track_and_field "Track and field") |
| Event | [Marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") |
| Club | Tartu Ulikooli Akadeemiline Spordiklubi[[2]](#cite_note-nbc-2) |

| Personal best | 2:40:30 (2015)[[3]](#cite_note-iaaf-3) |

**Lily Luik** (born 14 October 1985) is an Estonian [long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_running "Long-distance running").[[3]](#cite_note-iaaf-3) She competed in the [marathon event](/wiki/2015_World_Championships_in_Athletics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_marathon "2015 World Championships in Athletics – Women's marathon") at the [2015 World Championships in Athletics](/wiki/2015_World_Championships_in_Athletics "2015 World Championships in Athletics") in Beijing, China.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

She is one of an [identical triplet](/wiki/Multiple_birth "Multiple birth"), sister of [Liina](/wiki/Liina_Luik "Liina Luik") and [Leila](/wiki/Leila_Luik "Leila Luik"). All three qualified to participate in the marathon event representing [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") at the [2016 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2016_Summer_Olympics "2016 Summer Olympics") in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"). The triplets were professional dancers before Liina brought them to running.[[5]](#cite_note-nbc2-5)
